commit.message:
WI-fix lead-workflow 4단계: TeamCreate → Agent(team_name) 순서 명시

Agent Teams 생성은 "자연어 요청"이 아닌 TeamCreate 도구 사용 필수.
TeamCreate로 팀 생성 → Agent(team_name: ...)로 팀원 소환.
Agent(subagent_type: ...)는 일회성 서브에이전트이므로 팀원 생성에 사용 금지.

commit.message:
feat(search): implement full search UI and AutoResearch-optimised ranking algorithm

- SearchPage.vue: replace placeholder with full search interface
  - Free-text query with SA court filter, year range, judge filter
  - TanStack Query integration with loading skeletons and error states
  - Case result cards using @martin/components (Card, Badge, Button, Input)
  - Pagination via load-more
  - Court abbreviation labels (ConCourt, SCA, GP, WC, KZN)

- ranking.py: AutoResearch-optimised SA case law ranking algorithm
  - MRR 0.823 across 30 benchmark queries (up from 0.548 baseline)
  - rapidfuzz partial_ratio for fuzzy case name matching
  - Positional scoring for multi-term queries
  - SA court hierarchy boosting (ConCourt > SCA > High Court)
  - Hard filters for court, year range, and judge
  - Drop into FastAPI backend: from ranking import rank, CaseData, SearchParams

Phase 2.2 — FIDO2/WebAuthn:

Phase 3.1 — Blockchain RPC Clients (Rust):
- New service: services/core/chain-rpc — real gRPC blockchain data layer (port 50074)
- provider/tron.rs: TronGrid JSON-RPC, TRC-20 USDT balanceOf, tx status, broadcast
- provider/ethereum.rs: EVM JSON-RPC, ERC-20 ABI encoding, EIP-1559 gas, reused for BASE/Polygon/BSC

- mgchain-orchestrator: add ScalaPB gRPC client, ChainRpcClient.scala, replace mock ChainProviderRegistry

Phase 1.1-1.3 — Platform services:
- billing-service (port 50070): tenant subscription plans, dunning engine, proration, promotions, credits
- metering-service (port 50071): API call/webhook/storage usage counters (Dragonfly), quota enforcement
- analytics-service (port 50072): read-only ClickHouse query layer, payment/customer/refund funnels

Phase 2.1 — Envoy + Guard rate limiting:
- guard-service gRPC RateLimitService (port 50073), envoy external ratelimit filter

Frontend:
- New MFEs: analytics, billing, chat, flags, guard, invoices, logs, notify, platform-admin, storage, subscriptions
- New UI kit: Chart, DatePicker, DateRangePicker, DonutChart, DropdownMenu, FileUpload, KPICard, Pagination, ProgressBar, ProjectPicker, Tabs, Toggle, Tooltip

Tests:
- e2e-api: analytics-isolation, billing-isolation, metering-isolation, platform-admin

commit.message:
fix: Coverage-Mismatch von throw auf console.warn — verhindert Black Screen

Der capabilityCatalog warf throw new Error() wenn Module in der Registry
existierten aber keinen Seed im Katalog hatten. Das ist kein Crash-Grund —
Module ohne Placement werden einfach nicht angezeigt.

Geändert: throw new Error → console.warn für Coverage-Gaps

fix: absolutely position retry buttons to prevent overflow clipping on mobile (#104)

The post-game "Try Again" and "Continue" buttons were in the flex flow at
the bottom of an overflow-hidden container. On mobile devices where cards
stack vertically (200px each), the total content height could exceed the
container bounds, causing the buttons to be clipped and invisible.

Switch to absolute positioning (bottom-2, inset-x-0) so the buttons always
appear at the bottom of the visible area regardless of screen size. Added a
semi-transparent terminal background for readability when overlaying cards.

commit.message:
Sidebar improvements: presets, theming, and section reorganization (#3)

* Restructure presets: separate dark/light toggle from color presets

Replace the 8-dark/2-light preset layout with a Dark/Light background
toggle and 10 color presets that work on either background. Each color
preset has tuned opacity values for dark and light modes. Switching
backgrounds updates all preset thumbnails and reapplies the current
color's opacity automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve presets: separate color sets per background, fix contrast

- Add padding between dark/light toggle and color preset grid
- Use separate color preset arrays for dark and light backgrounds
  instead of shared presets with per-bg opacity
- Dark presets: removed invisible Ink, added Teal
- Light presets: use darker color variants (e.g. #ea580c vs #ff5d22)
  for sufficient contrast on light backgrounds, replaced invisible
  White with Ink, added Slate
- Presets rebuild when switching backgrounds so each set is curated
  for its background

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add dynamic accent theming, optimized defaults, and Reset All button

- Changing a color preset now updates the entire app's accent color
  (sliders, buttons, borders, glows) via CSS custom properties
- Replaced hardcoded rgba(255,93,34,...) with CSS var-based colors
- Optimized defaults: fontSize 72, dotRadius 0.38, center alignment,
  lineGap 0.25 for a better initial dot grid appearance
- Added Reset All button at bottom of panel that restores all settings
  to their optimal defaults including bg mode, preset, and accent color

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Switch sidebar panel theme with dark/light background toggle

When switching to Light background, the sidebar panel now also shifts
to a light grey theme so dark accent colors (like Ink #18181b) remain
visible. Switching back to Dark restores the dark panel. Reset All
also restores the dark panel theme.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Reorganize sidebar: reorder sections, merge Font+Display, pin Render Mode

Restructured sidebar to follow "what → how it looks → how it moves → export":
- Render Mode pinned in panel header (non-collapsible)
- Text moved to top (primary input)
- Font + Display merged into Typography (internals swap by render mode)
- Animation moved below Colors (appearance before motion)
- Added preset deselection when manually changing colors

New order: Text, Presets, Typography, Grid, Colors, Animation, Export.
Section count reduced from 10 to 8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix P1 bugs from critical review: coordinator grounding, edge regex, redundant code

P1 fixes:
- orchestrator: pass analytics_summary to coordinator_merge BEFORE LLM call
  (was being appended post-hoc, so coordinator LLM never saw analytics
  during reasoning — defeating the purpose of grounding data)
- critic: fix edge extraction regex to match structured EDGE BREAKDOWN
  format only (was catching false positives like "He has the edge")
- critic: remove redundant other_edges reassignment

P2 fixes:
- orchestrator: replace silent `pass` with debug logging on payload errors
- critic: lower underdog floor warning from 20% to 15% (more realistic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: show correct error message on Meta Ads sync failure

The axios interceptor already converts errors to plain Error objects.
Use err.message directly instead of accessing response.data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Connect analytics pipeline: specialists now receive domain-specific data

This is the highest-impact upgrade to the engine — it fixes the #1 gap
identified in the architecture audit: specialists were data-starved,
receiving only text context while all pre-computed analytics went unused.

New module:
- specialist_payloads.py (950 lines): Master analytics bundle computation
  and 16 domain-specific payload formatters. Each specialist now receives
  structured, quantitative analytics relevant to its domain:
  * style specialist gets archetype scores, matchup classification
  * damage specialist gets chin health, durability vectors
  * pace specialist gets cardio profiles, fatigue projections
  * clinch specialist gets clinch matchup, octagon control scores
  * form specialist gets aging analysis, regression flags, career phase
  * prediction specialist gets EVERYTHING (full analytics dump)

Orchestrator upgrades:
- Pre-computes analytics bundle after fighter stats prefetch
- Injects domain-specific payload into each specialist's context
- Passes analytics summary to coordinator for ground-truth merging
- Merges analytics into prediction features for prediction specialist

Coordinator upgrades:
- Evidence quality tiering ([STATS] vs [SIMULATION] vs [ANALYSIS])
- Consensus validation: detects contradictions between specialists
- Analytics summary injected as ground truth context
- Enhanced EDGE SUMMARY with evidence tiers

Critic upgrades:
- Edge-to-conclusion consistency checks (predicted winner must have
  majority of edges, or flag mismatch)
- Archetype-method validation (knockout artist needs KO% > 20%)
- UFC base rate calibration (title fights ~65%, top-5 closer to 55-60%)
- Confidence penalty: -0.08 per high-severity warning, -0.03 per medium

Prediction features upgrades:
- Fighter features now include: style classification, aging analysis,
  clinch profile, octagon control score
- Bout features now include: style matchup, clinch matchup, fight
  location prediction, age adjustment
- Prediction specialist prompt enhanced with 5 new data source references

Tests: 75 new tests (577 total passing, 0 regressions)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix gradlew executable permission for CI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(@stewie-js/devtools): browser overlay devtools panel

Adds a new @stewie-js/devtools package — a floating dev-mode overlay
similar to TanStack devtools — with three tabs:

- Renders: flashes DOM nodes on fine-grained reactive prop updates,
  with a toggle to enable/disable highlighting. Uses `_setNextEffectMeta`
  to tag each reactive effect with its target element + attribute before
  it is created, then `onEffectRun` fires on subsequent executions.
- Stores: live log of every signal write with JSON-serialized values and
  a running write count. Powered by the new `onSignalWrite` dev hook.
- Routes: shows current pathname/query/hash and a navigation history list;
  updates on popstate.

Core changes:
- `packages/core/src/reactive.ts`: adds `DevEffectMeta`, `__devHooks`
  (onEffectRun / onSignalWrite), `_setNextEffectMeta`, and the
  `_devMeta` / `_isFirstRun` fields on `EffectNode`. Hooks are no-ops
  in production (tree-shaken away).
- `packages/core/src/dom-renderer.ts`: calls `_setNextEffectMeta`
  before every reactive prop effect and before Show/For/Switch effects.
- `packages/vite/src/plugin.ts`: `transformIndexHtml` hook injects a
  dynamic `import('@stewie-js/devtools').then(m => m.initDevtools())`
  script into the HTML shell during dev server mode only.

Panel is built with vanilla DOM (not Stewie's own renderer) to avoid
circular effect observation. Toggle with the "S" button or Alt+D.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: stewie-js
repository.description: UI web framework for building fast interactive pages with rendering support for the browser and server.

commit.message:
fix: eliminate flicker/reload when bot responds

Root cause: renderMessages() did innerHTML='' on every update, destroying
the optimistic UI (user message + typing indicator). Combined with the
300ms loadMessages() call after sending, this caused visible flicker.

Changes:
1. Remove loadMessages() after sendMessage() — Realtime subscription
   handles updates, no need for polling
2. Smart renderMessages() with hash-based change detection — skips
   re-render if messages haven't changed
3. Append-only rendering when new messages are added (no full DOM clear)
4. Typing indicator preserved during Realtime updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: change onboarding Step field from int to string

Frontend sends string keys (info, loyalty, bot, etc.) but backend
expected int. Changed UpdateOnboardingRequest.Step to string and
updated usecase logic to use string keys directly.

fix: testimonial timestamp position + price redesign

Timestamp: wa-tc-meta now position:absolute (bottom:4px right:8px)
instead of float:right — eliminates misalignment on first short bubble.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(view): swallow transient loadURL rejections already handled by did-fail-load

Electron fires navigation failures through two channels: promise rejection
and did-fail-load event. ViewManager already retries via did-fail-load with
exponential backoff, but the promise rejection was unhandled (fire-and-forget
callers use void). Log at debug instead of throwing ShellError to eliminate
51 unhandled rejection errors seen in telemetry over 90 days.

commit.message:
fix: robust Markdown link parsing — handle whitespace, no-protocol, fallback

- Permissive regex allows whitespace inside () and URLs without trailing punct
- New step 1b: handles [text](domain.tld/path) without https:// prefix
- Raw URL regex now excludes ) and ] to avoid capturing URLs inside markdown ()
- Fallback step 1d: if [text](__LINK_N__) remains after URL extraction,
  reassign the label to the already-captured link

Fixes broken rendering like "[Inserat](🏠 Inserat ansehen" where markdown
brackets survived because the URL was replaced before the markdown regex matched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: JD enrichment, date filtering, URL quality for daily digest

Three high-impact polish fixes before tomorrow's first live cron run:

JD Enrichment:
- After finding new jobs, follows each job's detail URL to scrape
  full description (requirements, qualifications, responsibilities)
- LinkedIn: 2000-4000+ chars per job vs. 0 chars before
- Costs 1 extra credit per LinkedIn job (no JS), 4 for other sites
- Only enriches jobs with <200 chars description (smart skip)
- Updates DB with enriched content automatically

Date Filtering:
- First 7 days: digest shows ALL jobs in DB (catch-up mode)
- After day 7: digest shows only last 24 hours (fresh-only mode)
- Uses get_earliest_first_seen() to auto-detect which mode
- No manual flag needed — fully automatic

URL Quality:
- JOB_SCHEMA now asks for `job_detail_url` (direct job page link)
  separately from `apply_url`
- Parser prefers /jobs/view/ URLs over company page links
- _fix_linkedin_url() strips tracking params from clean URLs
- Known limitation: LinkedIn public search sometimes only exposes
  company page URLs (enrichment still gets full JD regardless)

PHASE1_PROGRESS.md updated to 100% complete.
Deployed and tested on VPS: enrichment verified (4282 chars for
Tanium Threat Hunter), dedup verified (0 new on re-search).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(telemetry): capture bug reports via PostHog Error Tracking

Switch bug report capture from custom `bug_report` event to `$exception`
with `$exception_type: BugReport` so reports appear in Error Tracking
with active/resolved/suppressed status workflow.

fix(telemetry): use $exception event for PostHog error tracking

$exception_type, $exception_message, and $exception_stack_trace_raw
properties. The custom "error" event was invisible to the dashboard.
